name: Alan Freed
abstract: Albert James "Alan" Freed (December 15, 1921 – January 20, 1965) was an American disc jockey. He also produced and promoted large traveling concerts with various acts, helping to spread the importance of rock and roll music throughout North America, including popularizing the term "rock and roll".

about_quotes.speaker: R.C.H. Smith
about_quotes.evidence: I was living and working in New York when Alan Freed came out of, I think it was Cleveland, to break it up in New York... So Alan Freed had to just become Alan Freed and then he went on to be rock promoter

about_quotes.speaker: Lionel Reason
about_quotes.evidence: I just sat in the band and Paul Lee Barnes was the brain... Alan Freed was the outstanding, well, stand in front of the band.

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy McRae
about_quotes.evidence: I think I got a guy who did all the writing for Alan Freed... He did the music -- he did the writing for me.

about_quotes.speaker: Marty Morell
about_quotes.evidence: I saw the drummer up there playing. It was Alan Freed, I don’t know if you have any rock & roll fans.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marty Morell interviewed by Monk Rowe, Orlando, Florida, February 24, 2017

about_quotes.speaker: Panama Francis
about_quotes.evidence: Alan Freed picked it up from Al Benson, ‘cause he thought that’s what the music was you know.
about_quotes.doc_title: Panama Francis interviewed by Michael Woods, Los Angeles, California, September 1, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Harrison Verrett
about_quotes.evidence: Well, the rock and roll that came from Alan Freed, he does...
